# Reykjavik city guide

## Overview

Reykjavik is the world's northernmost capital, a tiny, colorful city perched on the edge of volcanic landscapes, glaciers, and the Northern Lights. It's the base camp for Iceland's otherworldly nature, but the city itself has incredible food, a surprisingly wild nightlife scene, and creative energy that defies its small size.

**Best time to visit**: Jun-Aug for midnight sun and hiking. Sep-Mar for Northern Lights and ice caves. Oct-Feb for cheapest flights.
**Currency**: Icelandic Krona (ISK). 138 ISK ≈ 1 USD. Credit cards accepted everywhere, even hot dog stands.
**Language tip**: Icelandic is beautifully complex. Everyone speaks perfect English. "Takk" (thank you) is all you need.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| 101 Reykjavik (Downtown) | Colorful, walkable | Laugavegur shopping, restaurants, nightlife | Walk from anywhere downtown |
| Old Harbour | Maritime, hip | Whale watching, seafood, Harpa, galleries | Walk from 101 (10 min) |
| Grandi (Vesturbær) | Creative, emerging | Omnom chocolate, Marshall House gallery, FlyOver | Walk from Old Harbour |
| Laugardalur | Residential, sporty | Geothermal pools, botanical garden, family activities | Bus 14 from downtown |
| Hlemmur/East Side | Trendy, foodie | Hlemmur Matholl food hall, craft bars, budget stays | Walk from 101 (10 min) |
| Seltjarnarnes | Quiet, coastal | Grottja lighthouse, coastal walks, local vibes | Bus 11 from downtown |
| Kopavogur | Suburban | Smáralind mall, natural pools, local feel | Bus from downtown (15 min) |
| Hafnarfjordur | Viking, quirky | Viking village, lava fields, hidden people tours | Bus 1 from downtown (25 min) |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Baejarins Beztu Pylsur | Hot dogs | 101 Downtown | $ | Iceland's most famous hot dog stand since 1937. Bill Clinton ate here. The "one with everything" is the order. | Eina med ollu (one with everything) |
| Grillid | Fine dining | 101 Downtown | $$$ | Top-floor restaurant in the Saga Hotel with panoramic views. Modern Icelandic cuisine with local ingredients. | Arctic char, lamb, tasting menu |
| Hlemmur Matholl | Food hall | Hlemmur | $-$$ | Reykjavik's food hall with stalls from Vietnamese pho to Icelandic lamb. Great lunch spot. | Skyr dessert, lamb soup, Vietnamese pho |
| Rok | Icelandic | 101 Downtown | $$ | Small plates of Icelandic ingredients cooked over fire. Smoked trout, lamb, fermented shark (if you dare). | Smoked arctic char, lamb skewers, rye bread ice cream |
| Cafe Loki | Traditional Icelandic | 101 Downtown | $$ | Right next to Hallgrimskirkja. The place to try traditional dishes: fermented shark, dried fish, rye bread. | Traditional Icelandic plate, lamb soup |
| Messinn | Seafood | 101 Downtown | $$ | Fish served in sizzling skillets. The catch of the day in butter and garlic is simple perfection. | Fish pan (catch of the day), plokkfiskur |
| Sandholt Bakery | Bakery | 101 Downtown | $$ | Reykjavik's best bakery. Sourdough, pastries, and strong coffee in a warm, inviting space. | Cinnamon roll, sourdough bread, latte |
| Saegreifinn (Sea Baron) | Seafood | Old Harbour | $$ | Lobster soup from a no-frills harbor shack. The soup is rich, creamy, and the reason people come. | Lobster soup, grilled seafood skewers |
| Apotek Kitchen and Bar | Modern Nordic | 101 Downtown | $$$ | Upscale dining in a former pharmacy. Beautifully plated Icelandic ingredients with cocktail expertise. | Lamb rack, cocktail pairing, weekend brunch |
| Bjarni Fel | Sports pub | 101 Downtown | $$ | For when you need pub food and cheap(er) beer. Burgers, wings, and football on screens. Local crowd. | Burger, happy hour beer |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hallgrimskirkja | Culture | 101 Downtown | $ | 30 min-1 hour | Iceland's iconic church with a tower observation deck. Basalt column design inspired by volcanic landscapes. |
| Blue Lagoon | Geothermal | Reykjanes (40 min) | $$$ | 2-3 hours | The iconic milky-blue geothermal pool. Book months ahead. The Silica Hotel premium package includes private lagoon. |
| Golden Circle | Day trip | South Iceland | $$-$$$ | Full day | Thingvellir (tectonic plates), Geysir (geothermal area), Gullfoss (waterfall). The essential Iceland day trip. |
| Northern Lights | Nature | Outside city | Free-$$$ | Evening (Sep-Mar) | Drive 30 minutes from city lights. Tours are convenient but self-driving gives flexibility. Check aurora forecast. |
| Harpa Concert Hall | Architecture | Old Harbour | Free-$$ | 30 min-1 hour | Stunning glass building on the waterfront. Free to walk through the lobby. Check for concerts. |
| Sky Lagoon | Geothermal | Kopavogur | $$$ | 2-3 hours | Newer, less crowded alternative to Blue Lagoon. Ocean-view infinity pool with a 7-step spa ritual. |
| Whale Watching | Nature | Old Harbour | $$$ | 3 hours | Humpback whales, dolphins, and puffins (summer). Several operators from the old harbor. |
| Snaefellsnes Peninsula | Day trip | West Iceland | $$ | Full day | "Iceland in miniature." Black church, Kirkjufell mountain, lava fields, glaciers. 2 hours from Reykjavik. |
| Perlan Museum | Museum | Oskjuhlid | $$ | 2 hours | Indoor ice cave, planetarium, nature exhibitions. The observation deck has 360-degree views of Reykjavik. |
| Laugavegur Shopping Street | Walk | 101 Downtown | Free | 1-2 hours | The main drag. Shops, galleries, cafes, and bars. Most of Reykjavik's dining and nightlife is here or nearby. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Laugavegur Bar Crawl | Bars | 101 Downtown | $$-$$$ | Friday and Saturday nights, the whole street transforms. Bar hop starting after midnight. Icelanders start late. |
| Kaffibarinn | Bar | 101 Downtown | $$ | Damon Albarn co-owned it. Eclectic music, artsy crowd, and a vibe that's quintessentially Reykjavik. |
| Lebowski Bar | Themed bar | 101 Downtown | $$ | Big Lebowski themed. White Russians, bowling alley aesthetic, fun crowd. |
| Paloma | Club/bar | 101 Downtown | $$ | Rooftop terrace and basement dance floor. The closest thing to a "real" club in Reykjavik. |
| Micro Bar | Craft beer | 101 Downtown | $$ | Reykjavik's best craft beer bar. Icelandic microbrews on tap, knowledgeable staff. |

## Transit tips

- **Walking**: Downtown Reykjavik is tiny and entirely walkable. You don't need transit within 101.
- **Rental car**: Essential for anything outside the city. Book ahead in summer. 4WD for highlands and winter.
- **Streto bus**: City bus system. Not frequent but covers Reykjavik and suburbs. Use the Streto app.
- **No Uber**: Uber doesn't operate in Iceland. Taxis exist but are expensive (everything in Iceland is expensive).
- **Airport**: Keflavik (KEF) is 45 minutes from Reykjavik. FlyBus or rental car. No train connection.
- **Pro tip**: Gas stations have the best cheap hot dogs and coffee. Also useful for free WiFi on road trips.
- **Cost warning**: Iceland is very expensive. Budget $100-150/day minimum. Happy hours (3-6 PM) are your friend.
- **Road conditions**: Check road.is before driving. Weather changes fast, especially in winter.

## Useful Icelandic phrases

| English | Icelandic | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Hallo | HAH-lo |
| Thank you | Takk | tahk |
| Cheers! | Skal! | skowl |
| Yes/No | Ja/Nei | yow / nay |
| How much? | Hvad kostar? | kvath KOS-tar |
| Beautiful | Fallegt | FAL-lekt |
| Goodbye | Bless | bless |
| Where is...? | Hvar er...? | kvar air |
